name: loop-kit-dev
description: [DEV] Turn-gated loop plugin that keeps one turn alive until explicit user stop and applies phase protocols from turn-gate.

interface.shortDescription: [DEV] Turn-gated continuity with execution, refinement, review, and readiness phase protocols driven from one main surface.
interface.longDescription: [DEV] Provides a narrow operational plugin where turn-gate is the main user-facing surface. It keeps one turn alive until the user explicitly ends the work, runs each flow through preparation, work, verification, and reporting, records user-message intake and planned-flow design as an operational-preparation flow when it owns session plan, flow-list, scope, or approval-boundary artifacts, and treats execution planned flows as cohesive reviewable or commit-sized change units rather than phase checklists. Self-drive is a separate overlay reference for prepared autonomous sequences.
defaultPrompt: Keep this turn alive until I explicitly stop and reopen the next flow with explicit choices.
Run each turn-gated flow as preparation, work, verification, and reporting.
Treat planned flows as cohesive reviewable or commit-sized change units, not analysis/work/verification phase slices and not only direct user-visible value.
Keep pure final QA, consistency checks, verification-result reporting, and commit-readiness reporting out of the planned flow list unless they own a distinct artifact/change unit.
For user-message preparation, use deep-interview alignment and turn the result into a planned flow list before execution.
Treat user-message intake and planned-flow design as an operational-preparation flow when it owns session plan, flow-list, scope, or approval-boundary artifacts.

Collect enough information during user-message preparation to execute planned flows with clear boundaries, non-goals, verification expectations, expected risky actions, and user-gated approval checkpoints.
For existing-flow preparation, inspect scope, current state, target files, and verification expectations before work.
Use turn-gate as the main controller and choose the right phase protocol for the current phase.
Handle this as a turn-gated loop where refinement, review, and readiness are phase protocols for the current phase.
Keep turn-gate's loop contract independent from self-drive overlay behavior.
When a prepared sequence explicitly uses self-drive, apply the self-drive overlay contract instead of repeating its conditions in turn-gate.
Do not expose direct loop entrypoints; drive the work through turn-gate.
